summary: DHH announces that 'Agile Web Development with Rails' has entered its third printing less than a month after its paper release. The book is ranked #83 overall on Amazon and #7 in the computer department. He expresses satisfaction at the commercial success and frames it as validation that Rails has arrived as a serious framework.
key_points: The Rails book hit three printings in under a month, indicating unexpectedly high demand
Amazon ranked it #83 overall and #7 in computers, showing mainstream crossover appeal
DHH frames the book's sales as a signal to the broader tech world that Rails is a legitimate platform
money_quotes: It's intensely satisfying to see a book carrying your name on the spine slam dunk in sales.
Thanks once more to everyone that bought the book and helped send a strong signal to the world that Rails is real.
key_insight: The rapid commercial success of the first Rails book served as early mainstream validation that the framework had real momentum beyond the early-adopter community.
